key_insight: The paper introduces Lie-Algebra Attention, where each token is a bare matrix Lie group element and attention scores are computed directly from the logarithm of relative group poses.
engineering_relevance: This gives a closed-form, parameter-efficient, and equivariant attention mechanism that works on matrix Lie groups including non-compact affine groups relevant to geometry-aware sequence modeling.
method: Token = group element g_i with no feature payload or external representation
Score: s_ij = -||log(g_i^{-1} g_j)||_lambda^2 / tau using a block-weighted Frobenius norm
evidence_signal: On sequence-completion tasks over SE(2), SO(3), and Aff(2), the closed-form score matched an MLP kernel on the same invariant, outperformed it on SE(2), used 50–80x fewer score parameters, and preserved invariance far better than a vector-token baseline.
watch_item: The method depends on a valid logarithm chart containing the relative poses, so implementation must handle chart coverage, branch choices, and numerical stability near singular regions.
paper_title: The Token Is a Group Element: On Lie-Algebra Attention over Matrix Lie Groups
